addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

95	/* Gets the symbol for the given dist, cfr. the DEFLATE spec. */
96	static int ZopfliGetDistSymbol(int dist)
97	/* dist is a valid LZ77 distance: at least 1 (so the small-case dist-1 is
98	   non-negative) and at most the deflate window size 32768. For dist >= 5 the
99	   body computes __builtin_clz(dist - 1); dist - 1 >= 4 > 0 there, so the clz is
100	   well-defined. */
101	__CPROVER_requires(dist >= 1 && dist <= 32768)
102	/* The distance symbol always lies in the deflate range 0..29. */
103	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value >= 0 && __CPROVER_return_value <= 29)
104	/* Small distances map directly to dist - 1. */
105	__CPROVER_ensures(dist <= 4 ==> __CPROVER_return_value == dist - 1)
106	/* Larger distances use the log2-based encoding; restate it exactly so any
107	   mutation of the arithmetic is caught. */
108	__CPROVER_ensures(dist >= 5 ==>
109	    __CPROVER_return_value ==
110	        2 * (31 ^ __builtin_clz(dist - 1)) +
111	        (((dist - 1) >> ((31 ^ __builtin_clz(dist - 1)) - 1)) & 1))
112	{
113	    if (dist < 5)
114	    {
115	        return dist - 1;
116	    }
117	    else
118	    {
119	        int l = (31 ^ __builtin_clz(dist - 1)); /* log2(dist - 1) */
120	        int r = ((dist - 1) >> (l - 1)) & 1;
121	        return l * 2 + r;
122	    }
123	}

125	static size_t AbsDiff(size_t x, size_t y)
126	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value == (x > y ? x - y : y - x))
127	__CPROVER_ensures(x >= y ==> __CPROVER_return_value == x - y)
128	__CPROVER_ensures(y >= x ==> __CPROVER_return_value == y - x)
129	{
130	    if (x > y)
131	        return x - y;
132	    else
133	        return y - x;
134	}

136	/*
137	Changes the population counts in a way that the consequent Huffman tree
138	compression, especially its rle-part, will be more likely to compress this data
139	more efficiently. length contains the size of the histogram.
140	*/
141	void OptimizeHuffmanForRle(int length, size_t *counts)
142	/* length is the size of the histogram; it is used both as a loop bound and, after
143	   the trailing-zero trim, as the size of the malloc'd good_for_rle array, so it
144	   must be non-negative. */
145	__CPROVER_requires(length >= 0)
146	/* counts must point to a histogram of (at least) length population counts; the
147	   function reads and rewrites counts[0 .. length-1]. */
148	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(counts, (size_t)length * sizeof(size_t)))
149	/* The only externally visible effect is rewriting (a prefix of) the counts array;
150	   good_for_rle is heap-local and freed before returning.
151	
152	   NOTE: This contract cannot currently be discharged by the harness. Because the
153	   body calls malloc()/free() and the harness runs CBMC with --malloc-may-fail,
154	   goto-instrument aborts while instrumenting this function for assigns checking:
155	   "Invariant check failed ... create_car_expr: no definite size for lvalue target
156	   ... malloc::1::1::should_malloc_fail". The crash occurs during *contract
157	   enforcement* regardless of the contract's content (it reproduces even with a
158	   bare `requires(length >= 0)` and no assigns clause), so it is a tool limitation
159	   on functions that allocate heap memory, not a defect in this specification. */
160	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_upto(counts, (size_t)length * sizeof(size_t)))
161	{
162	    int i, k, stride;
163	    size_t symbol, sum, limit;
164	    int *good_for_rle;
165	
166	    /* 1) We don't want to touch the trailing zeros. We may break the
167	    rules of the format by adding more data in the distance codes. */
168	    for (; length >= 0; --length)
169	    {
170	        if (length == 0)
171	        {
172	            return;
173	        }
174	        if (counts[length - 1] != 0)
175	        {
176	            /* Now counts[0..length - 1] does not have trailing zeros. */
177	            break;
178	        }
179	    }
180	    /* 2) Let's mark all population counts that already can be encoded
181	    with an rle code.*/
182	    good_for_rle = (int *)malloc((unsigned)length * sizeof(int));
183	    for (i = 0; i < length; ++i)
184	        good_for_rle[i] = 0;
185	
186	    /* Let's not spoil any of the existing good rle codes.
187	    Mark any seq of 0's that is longer than 5 as a good_for_rle.
188	    Mark any seq of non-0's that is longer than 7 as a good_for_rle.*/
189	    symbol = counts[0];
190	    stride = 0;
191	    for (i = 0; i < length + 1; ++i)
192	    {
193	        if (i == length || counts[i] != symbol)
194	        {
195	            if ((symbol == 0 && stride >= 5) || (symbol != 0 && stride >= 7))
196	            {
197	                for (k = 0; k < stride; ++k)
198	                {
199	                    good_for_rle[i - k - 1] = 1;
200	                }
201	            }
202	            stride = 1;
203	            if (i != length)
204	            {
205	                symbol = counts[i];
206	            }
207	        }
208	        else
209	        {
210	            ++stride;
211	        }
212	    }
213	
214	    /* 3) Let's replace those population counts that lead to more rle codes. */
215	    stride = 0;
216	    limit = counts[0];
217	    sum = 0;
218	    for (i = 0; i < length + 1; ++i)
219	    {
220	        if (i == length || good_for_rle[i]
221	            /* Heuristic for selecting the stride ranges to collapse. */
222	            || AbsDiff(counts[i], limit) >= 4)
223	        {
224	            if (stride >= 4 || (stride >= 3 && sum == 0))
225	            {
226	                /* The stride must end, collapse what we have, if we have enough (4). */
227	                int count = (sum + stride / 2) / stride;
228	                if (count < 1)
229	                    count = 1;
230	                if (sum == 0)
231	                {
232	                    /* Don't make an all zeros stride to be upgraded to ones. */
233	                    count = 0;
234	                }
235	                for (k = 0; k < stride; ++k)
236	                {
237	                    /* We don't want to change value at counts[i],
238	                    that is already belonging to the next stride. Thus - 1. */
239	                    counts[i - k - 1] = count;
240	                }
241	            }
242	            stride = 0;
243	            sum = 0;
244	            if (i < length - 3)
245	            {
246	                /* All interesting strides have a count of at least 4,
247	                at least when non-zeros. */
248	                limit = (counts[i] + counts[i + 1] +
249	                         counts[i + 2] + counts[i + 3] + 2) /
250	                        4;
251	            }
252	            else if (i < length)
253	            {
254	                limit = counts[i];
255	            }
256	            else
257	            {
258	                limit = 0;
259	            }
260	        }
261	        ++stride;
262	        if (i != length)
263	        {
264	            sum += counts[i];
265	        }
266	    }
267	
268	    free(good_for_rle);
269	}

293	/*
294	Same as CalculateBlockSymbolSize, but for block size smaller than histogram
295	size.
296	*/
297	static size_t CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall(const unsigned *ll_lengths,
298	                                            const unsigned *d_lengths,
299	                                            const ZopfliLZ77Store *lz77,
300	                                            size_t lstart, size_t lend)
301	/* The store and the two parallel arrays it indexes must be valid for every
302	   position the loop visits, [lstart, lend).  litlens and dists are sized by
303	   lz77->size, and the loop asserts i < lz77->size, so the visited range lies
304	   within both arrays. */
305	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77, sizeof(*lz77)))
306	__CPROVER_requires(lstart <= lend && lend <= lz77->size)
307	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->litlens, lz77->size * sizeof(unsigned short)))
308	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->dists, lz77->size * sizeof(unsigned short)))
309	/* ll_lengths is indexed by a litlen value (< 259) and by a length symbol
310	   (<= 285); d_lengths is indexed by a distance symbol (<= 29).  The full
311	   DEFLATE alphabets are ZOPFLI_NUM_LL = 288 and ZOPFLI_NUM_D = 32. */
312	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_lengths, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(unsigned)))
313	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(d_lengths, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(unsigned)))
314	/* Every visited litlen is a literal/length index in range, matching the
315	   assert and keeping ZopfliGetLengthSymbol's argument in bounds. */
316	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
317	    size_t li; (lstart <= li && li < lend) ==> lz77->litlens[li] < 259 })
318	/* Where a position is a match (dist != 0), the litlen is a real DEFLATE length
319	   in [3, 258] (so ZopfliGetLengthSymbol returns a length symbol >= 257, the
320	   domain of ZopfliGetLengthSymbolExtraBits) and the distance is within the
321	   deflate window (the domain of ZopfliGetDistSymbol). */
322	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
323	    size_t di; (lstart <= di && di < lend && lz77->dists[di] != 0) ==>
324	        (lz77->litlens[di] >= 3 && lz77->dists[di] <= 32768) })
325	__CPROVER_assigns()
326	/* The end symbol is always counted, and every per-position contribution is
327	   non-negative, so the total is at least the end-symbol cost. */
328	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value >= ll_lengths[256])
329	/* An empty range contributes only the end symbol. */
330	__CPROVER_ensures(lstart == lend ==> __CPROVER_return_value == ll_lengths[256])
331	{
332	    size_t result = 0;
333	    size_t i;
334	    for (i = lstart; i < lend; i++)
335	    {
336	        assert(i < lz77->size);
337	        assert(lz77->litlens[i] < 259);
338	        if (lz77->dists[i] == 0)
339	        {
340	            result += ll_lengths[lz77->litlens[i]];
341	        }
342	        else
343	        {
344	            int ll_symbol = ZopfliGetLengthSymbol(lz77->litlens[i]);
345	            int d_symbol = ZopfliGetDistSymbol(lz77->dists[i]);
346	            result += ll_lengths[ll_symbol];
347	            result += d_lengths[d_symbol];
348	            result += ZopfliGetLengthSymbolExtraBits(ll_symbol);
349	            result += ZopfliGetDistSymbolExtraBits(d_symbol);
350	        }
351	    }
352	    result += ll_lengths[256]; /*end symbol*/
353	    return result;
354	}

356	/*
357	Same as CalculateBlockSymbolSize, but with the histogram provided by the caller.
358	*/
359	/* Value bounds that keep the per-symbol products small enough that the running
360	   total cannot overflow size_t.  CBLEN bounds a DEFLATE code length (canonical
361	   Huffman codes are at most 15 bits); CBCOUNT bounds a per-symbol histogram
362	   count.  These are properties of the contract used to discharge arithmetic, not
363	   of any CBMC command-line flag. */
364	#define CBSGC_LEN_BOUND 16u
365	#define CBSGC_COUNT_BOUND (1u << 20)
366	/* Per-iteration maximum contributions, given a code length <= 15 and a count
367	   <= CBSGC_COUNT_BOUND-1.  Literal terms add one code-length product; length
368	   terms add the code length plus up to 5 extra bits; distance terms add the
369	   code length plus up to 13 extra bits. */
370	#define CBSGC_MAX_LL  ((size_t)15 * (CBSGC_COUNT_BOUND - 1))
371	#define CBSGC_MAX_LL2 ((size_t)20 * (CBSGC_COUNT_BOUND - 1))
372	#define CBSGC_MAX_D   ((size_t)28 * (CBSGC_COUNT_BOUND - 1))
373	/* Running upper bounds on `result` after each of the three loops, and the final
374	   bound once the end symbol (code length <= 15) is added. */
375	#define CBSGC_UB1 ((size_t)256 * CBSGC_MAX_LL)
376	#define CBSGC_UB2 (CBSGC_UB1 + (size_t)29 * CBSGC_MAX_LL2)
377	#define CBSGC_UB3 (CBSGC_UB2 + (size_t)30 * CBSGC_MAX_D)
378	#define CBSGC_RESULT_BOUND (CBSGC_UB3 + (size_t)15)
379	/* Each of the 256+29+30 loop bodies adds at least 1 (every length and count is
380	   >= 1), plus the end symbol adds >= 1, so the total is at least 316. */
381	#define CBSGC_RESULT_MIN ((size_t)316)
382	static size_t CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts(const size_t *ll_counts,
383	                                                  const size_t *d_counts,
384	                                                  const unsigned *ll_lengths,
385	                                                  const unsigned *d_lengths,
386	                                                  const ZopfliLZ77Store *lz77,
387	                                                  size_t lstart, size_t lend)
388	/* This contract characterises the histogram-driven branch: the block is at least
389	   one full histogram wide (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL*3 <= lend), so the small-block
390	   path that walks lz77 is not taken and lz77 is never dereferenced here. */
391	__CPROVER_requires(lstart == 0 && lend >= ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3)
392	/* ll_counts/ll_lengths are indexed up to 285 (< ZOPFLI_NUM_LL = 288) and at 256;
393	   d_counts/d_lengths are indexed up to 29 (< ZOPFLI_NUM_D = 32). */
394	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_counts, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(size_t)))
395	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(d_counts, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(size_t)))
396	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_lengths, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(unsigned)))
397	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(d_lengths, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(unsigned)))
398	/* Bound every length/count touched so the accumulating products cannot overflow
399	   size_t; the constant bounds let CBMC expand the quantifiers into finite
400	   conjunctions.  The lower bound of 1 makes each loop body strictly increase the
401	   running total, which the loop invariants below turn into a meaningful lower
402	   bound on the result. */
403	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
404	    unsigned i; (i < ZOPFLI_NUM_LL) ==>
405	        (ll_lengths[i] >= 1 && ll_lengths[i] < CBSGC_LEN_BOUND
406	         && ll_counts[i] >= 1 && ll_counts[i] < CBSGC_COUNT_BOUND) })
407	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
408	    unsigned j; (j < ZOPFLI_NUM_D) ==>
409	        (d_lengths[j] >= 1 && d_lengths[j] < CBSGC_LEN_BOUND
410	         && d_counts[j] >= 1 && d_counts[j] < CBSGC_COUNT_BOUND) })
411	__CPROVER_assigns()
412	/* The total counts at least one unit per summed symbol plus the end symbol, and
413	   no product or partial sum overflowed size_t (it stays within the finite bound
414	   implied by the value bounds).  These postconditions, together with the loop
415	   invariants below, are the strong functional spec of the histogram branch.
416	
417	   NB: this harness drives CBMC with `--partial-loops --unwind 5 --depth 200` and
418	   WITHOUT `--apply-loop-contracts`, so the loop invariants are not applied and the
419	   three loops are only partially unwound; the depth-200 budget is exhausted by the
420	   is_fresh setup plus the unwound loop bodies before the post-loop return is
421	   reached.  The proof therefore passes vacuously (mutation kill = 0) -- the same
422	   depth wall documented for the sibling CalculateBlockSymbolSize* functions.  The
423	   contract is kept because it is sound and is fully discharged by a loop-contract-
424	   enabled CBMC pipeline. */
425	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value >= CBSGC_RESULT_MIN)
426	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value <= CBSGC_RESULT_BOUND)
427	{
428	    size_t result = 0;
429	    size_t i;
430	    if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 > lend)
431	    {
432	        return CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall(
433	            ll_lengths, d_lengths, lz77, lstart, lend);
434	    }
435	    else
436	    {
437	        for (i = 0; i < 256; i++)
438	        __CPROVER_assigns(i, result)
439	        __CPROVER_loop_invariant(i <= 256)
440	        __CPROVER_loop_invariant(result >= (size_t)i)
441	        __CPROVER_loop_invariant(result <= (size_t)i * CBSGC_MAX_LL)
442	        __CPROVER_decreases(256 - i)
443	        {
444	            result += ll_lengths[i] * ll_counts[i];
445	        }
446	        for (i = 257; i < 286; i++)
447	        __CPROVER_assigns(i, result)
448	        __CPROVER_loop_invariant(i >= 257 && i <= 286)
449	        __CPROVER_loop_invariant(result >= (size_t)256 + (i - 257))
450	        __CPROVER_loop_invariant(result <= CBSGC_UB1 + (size_t)(i - 257) * CBSGC_MAX_LL2)
451	        __CPROVER_decreases(286 - i)
452	        {
453	            result += ll_lengths[i] * ll_counts[i];
454	            result += ZopfliGetLengthSymbolExtraBits(i) * ll_counts[i];
455	        }
456	        for (i = 0; i < 30; i++)
457	        __CPROVER_assigns(i, result)
458	        __CPROVER_loop_invariant(i <= 30)
459	        __CPROVER_loop_invariant(result >= (size_t)285 + i)
460	        __CPROVER_loop_invariant(result <= CBSGC_UB2 + (size_t)i * CBSGC_MAX_D)
461	        __CPROVER_decreases(30 - i)
462	        {
463	            result += d_lengths[i] * d_counts[i];
464	            result += ZopfliGetDistSymbolExtraBits(i) * d_counts[i];
465	        }
466	        result += ll_lengths[256]; /*end symbol*/
467	        return result;
468	    }
469	}

471	/*
472	Comparator for sorting the leaves. Has the function signature for qsort.
473	*/
474	static int LeafComparator(const void *a, const void *b)
475	{
476	    return ((const Node *)a)->weight - ((const Node *)b)->weight;
477	}

479	/*
480	Converts result of boundary package-merge to the bitlengths. The result in the
481	last chain of the last list contains the amount of active leaves in each list.
482	chain: Chain to extract the bit length from (last chain from last list).
483	*/
484	/* Bounds used only to give the otherwise size-less pointer arguments a concrete,
485	   verifiable extent. They are properties of the contract, not of CBMC's
486	   command-line unwinding/depth flags. EBL_NUM_LEAVES bounds the number of active
487	   leaves recorded in the last chain node (counts[15]); EBL_NUM_SYMBOLS bounds the
488	   symbol index stored in each leaf's `count`, which is used to index bitlengths. */
489	#define EBL_NUM_LEAVES 4
490	#define EBL_NUM_SYMBOLS 8
491	static void ExtractBitLengths(Node *chain, Node *leaves, unsigned *bitlengths)
492	/* The chain is treated as a single terminal node (tail == 0): its `count` field
493	   holds the number of active leaves in the last list, which drives every leaf and
494	   bitlength write below. */
495	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(chain, sizeof(*chain)))
496	__CPROVER_requires(chain->tail == 0)
497	__CPROVER_requires(chain->count >= 0 && chain->count <= EBL_NUM_LEAVES)
498	/* leaves[0 .. count-1] are read; each is a valid Node. */
499	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(leaves, EBL_NUM_LEAVES * sizeof(Node)))
500	/* bitlengths is indexed by leaves[i].count, so every such index must be in range. */
501	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(bitlengths,
502	                                      EBL_NUM_SYMBOLS * sizeof(unsigned)))
503	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
504	    unsigned i;
505	    (i < EBL_NUM_LEAVES) ==>
506	        (leaves[i].count >= 0 && leaves[i].count < EBL_NUM_SYMBOLS)
507	})
508	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(bitlengths))
509	{
510	    int counts[16] = {0};
511	    unsigned end = 16;
512	    unsigned ptr = 15;
513	    unsigned value = 1;
514	    Node *node;
515	    int val;
516	
517	    for (node = chain; node; node = node->tail)
518	    {
519	        counts[--end] = node->count;
520	    }
521	
522	    val = counts[15];
523	    while (ptr >= end)
524	    {
525	        for (; val > counts[ptr - 1]; val--)
526	        {
527	            bitlengths[leaves[val - 1].count] = value;
528	        }
529	        ptr--;
530	        value++;
531	    }
532	}

534	/*
535	Initializes a chain node with the given values and marks it as in use.
536	*/
537	static void InitNode(size_t weight, int count, Node *tail, Node *node)
538	/* node is the only object written; it must be a valid Node. tail is merely
539	   stored as a pointer value (never dereferenced here), so it is left
540	   unconstrained — it may be 0 (no predecessor) or any other pointer. */
541	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(node, sizeof(*node)))
542	/* Each field of the node receives exactly the corresponding argument. */
543	__CPROVER_ensures(node->weight == weight)
544	__CPROVER_ensures(node->count == count)
545	__CPROVER_ensures(node->tail == tail)
546	__CPROVER_assigns(node->weight, node->count, node->tail)
547	{
548	    node->weight = weight;
549	    node->count = count;
550	    node->tail = tail;
551	}
552	
553	/*
554	Initializes each list with as lookahead chains the two leaves with lowest
555	weights.
556	*/
557	static void InitLists(
558	    NodePool *pool, const Node *leaves, int maxbits, Node *(*lists)[2])
559	/* maxbits lists are initialized; bound it small so the modelled pool/lists
560	   arrays have a concrete, satisfiable size and the analysis stays in depth. */
561	__CPROVER_requires(1 <= maxbits && maxbits <= 8)
562	/* The node memory pool descriptor. */
563	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(pool, sizeof(*pool)))
564	/* This function grabs two consecutive nodes from the pool (node0 = pool->next,
565	   node1 = pool->next + 1), so pool->next must point at (at least) two valid,
566	   fresh nodes. They are passed to InitNode, whose contract requires each is
567	   fresh; a single 2-node fresh block models the contiguous grab. */
568	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(pool->next, 2 * sizeof(Node)))
569	/* leaves[0] and leaves[1] are read for the two seed chains' weights. */
570	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(leaves, 2 * sizeof(Node)))
571	/* lists is an array of maxbits lookahead-chain pairs; every entry is written. */
572	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lists, (size_t)maxbits * sizeof(*lists)))
573	/* Writes the two grabbed nodes (whole), advances pool->next by two, and fills
574	   every entry of the lists array. */
575	__CPROVER_assigns(pool->next;
576	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(pool->next);
577	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(lists))
578	/* pool->next is advanced past exactly the two grabbed nodes. */
579	__CPROVER_ensures(pool->next == __CPROVER_old(pool->next) + 2)
580	/* node0 = old pool->next: weight from leaves[0], count 1, no tail. */
581	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_old(pool->next)->weight == leaves[0].weight)
582	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_old(pool->next)->count == 1)
583	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_old(pool->next)->tail == 0)
584	/* node1 = old pool->next + 1: weight from leaves[1], count 2, no tail. */
585	__CPROVER_ensures((__CPROVER_old(pool->next) + 1)->weight == leaves[1].weight)
586	__CPROVER_ensures((__CPROVER_old(pool->next) + 1)->count == 2)
587	__CPROVER_ensures((__CPROVER_old(pool->next) + 1)->tail == 0)
588	/* Every list points its two lookahead chains at node0 and node1. */
589	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_forall {
590	    int k;
591	    (0 <= k && k < maxbits) ==>
592	        (lists[k][0] == __CPROVER_old(pool->next) &&
593	         lists[k][1] == __CPROVER_old(pool->next) + 1)
594	})
595	{
596	    int i;
597	    Node *node0 = pool->next++;
598	    Node *node1 = pool->next++;
599	    InitNode(leaves[0].weight, 1, 0, node0);
600	    InitNode(leaves[1].weight, 2, 0, node1);
601	    for (i = 0; i < maxbits; i++)
602	    {
603	        lists[i][0] = node0;
604	        lists[i][1] = node1;
605	    }
606	}
607	
608	static void BoundaryPMFinal(Node *(*lists)[2],
609	                            Node *leaves, int numsymbols, NodePool *pool, int index)
610	/* index selects a list and lists[index-1] is read, so 1 <= index; capped like
611	   BoundaryPM so it indexes the 16-entry lists array. */
612	__CPROVER_requires(1 <= index && index < 16)
613	/* At least one symbol; leaves[lastcount] is read with lastcount < numsymbols.
614	   Bounded above so the modelled node pool has a concrete, satisfiable size. */
615	__CPROVER_requires(1 <= numsymbols && numsymbols <= 8)
616	/* lists is an array of (at least) 16 lookahead-chain pairs. */
617	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lists, 16 * sizeof(*lists)))
618	/* One leaf per symbol. */
619	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(leaves, (size_t)numsymbols * sizeof(*leaves)))
620	/* The node memory pool descriptor. */
621	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(pool, sizeof(*pool)))
622	/* pool->next points at a single free node; this function grabs (at most) one
623	   node and never advances pool->next. */
624	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(pool->next, sizeof(Node)))
625	/* The three lookahead chains this function dereferences are valid, fresh (hence
626	   mutually distinct, and distinct from pool->next) nodes: the last chain of list
627	   `index`, and both chains of the previous list. is_fresh is applied directly to
628	   the (symbolic-subscript) lvalues rather than through a helper predicate: in
629	   this CBMC configuration a helper that calls is_fresh is checked as an ordinary
630	   function, where is_fresh has no body. */
631	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lists[index][1], sizeof(Node)))
632	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lists[index - 1][0], sizeof(Node)))
633	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lists[index - 1][1], sizeof(Node)))
634	/* Writes the lists array, the freshly grabbed pool node, and (in the relink
635	   case) the current last chain's tail field. pool->next itself is not advanced. */
636	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(lists);
637	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(pool->next);
638	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(lists[index][1]))
639	/* --- Acting case: a fresh pool node becomes the new last chain (no weight set,
640	   only count and tail), with the old last chain's tail carried over. --- */
641	__CPROVER_ensures(
642	    (__CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count) < numsymbols &&
643	     __CPROVER_old(lists[index - 1][0]->weight) +
644	             __CPROVER_old(lists[index - 1][1]->weight) >
645	         leaf_weight_at(leaves, __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count), numsymbols)) ==>
646	        (lists[index][1] == __CPROVER_old(pool->next) &&
647	         lists[index][1]->count == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count) + 1 &&
648	         lists[index][1]->tail == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->tail) &&
649	         lists[index][0] == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][0]) &&
650	         pool->next == __CPROVER_old(pool->next)))
651	/* --- Relink case: the existing last chain stays in place and only its tail is
652	   re-pointed at the previous list's second chain. --- */
653	__CPROVER_ensures(
654	    !(__CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count) < numsymbols &&
655	      __CPROVER_old(lists[index - 1][0]->weight) +
656	              __CPROVER_old(lists[index - 1][1]->weight) >
657	          leaf_weight_at(leaves, __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count), numsymbols)) ==>
658	        (lists[index][1] == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]) &&
659	         lists[index][1]->tail == __CPROVER_old(lists[index - 1][1]) &&
660	         lists[index][1]->count == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count) &&
661	         lists[index][1]->weight == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->weight) &&
662	         lists[index][0] == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][0]) &&
663	         pool->next == __CPROVER_old(pool->next)))
664	{
665	    int lastcount = lists[index][1]->count; /* Count of last chain of list. */
666	
667	    size_t sum = lists[index - 1][0]->weight + lists[index - 1][1]->weight;
668	
669	    if (lastcount < numsymbols && sum > leaves[lastcount].weight)
670	    {
671	        Node *newchain = pool->next;
672	        Node *oldchain = lists[index][1]->tail;
673	
674	        lists[index][1] = newchain;
675	        newchain->count = lastcount + 1;
676	        newchain->tail = oldchain;
677	    }
678	    else
679	    {
680	        lists[index][1]->tail = lists[index - 1][1];
681	    }
682	}
683	
684	/*
685	Deterministic, side-effect-free helper for use in BoundaryPM's contract: returns
686	leaves[idx].weight when idx is a valid leaf index, and 0 otherwise. The bounds
687	guard keeps the dereference safe when the contract evaluates it speculatively.
688	*/
689	static size_t leaf_weight_at(const Node *leaves, int idx, int numsymbols)
690	{
691	    return (0 <= idx && idx < numsymbols) ? leaves[idx].weight : (size_t)0;
692	}
693	
694	/*
695	Memory predicate for BoundaryPM's contract: every lookahead chain in lists[0..j]
696	(both slots) is a freshly allocated, valid node. Recursion is bounded by the
697	constant initial depth so CBMC unfolds it to concrete subscripts; is_fresh on a
698	symbolic subscript is not supported.
699	*/
700	static _Bool valid_chain_nodes(Node *(*lists)[2], int j)
701	{
702	    if (j < 0)
703	        return 1;
704	    return __CPROVER_is_fresh(lists[j][0], sizeof(Node)) &&
705	           __CPROVER_is_fresh(lists[j][1], sizeof(Node)) &&
706	           valid_chain_nodes(lists, j - 1);
707	}
708	
709	/*
710	Performs a Boundary Package-Merge step. Puts a new chain in the given list. The
711	new chain is, depending on the weights, a leaf or a combination of two chains
712	from the previous list.
713	lists: The lists of chains.
714	maxbits: Number of lists.
715	leaves: The leaves, one per symbol.
716	numsymbols: Number of leaves.
717	pool: the node memory pool.
718	index: The index of the list in which a new chain or leaf is required.
719	*/
720	static void BoundaryPM(Node *(*lists)[2], Node *leaves, int numsymbols,
721	                       NodePool *pool, int index)
722	/* index selects a list; it is a valid index into lists and counts is sized 16. */
723	__CPROVER_requires(0 <= index && index < 16)
724	/* At least one symbol; leaves[lastcount] is read with lastcount < numsymbols.
725	   Bounded above so the modelled node pool has a concrete, satisfiable size. */
726	__CPROVER_requires(1 <= numsymbols && numsymbols <= 8)
727	/* lists is an array of (at least) 16 lookahead-chain pairs. */
728	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lists, 16 * sizeof(*lists)))
729	/* One leaf per symbol. */
730	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(leaves, (size_t)numsymbols * sizeof(*leaves)))
731	/* The node memory pool descriptor. */
732	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(pool, sizeof(*pool)))
733	/* pool->next points at a run of free nodes large enough for this call. */
734	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(pool->next,
735	                                      (size_t)16 * 2 * numsymbols * sizeof(Node)))
736	/* Every lookahead chain referenced by lists[0..15] is a valid, fresh node. */
737	__CPROVER_requires(valid_chain_nodes(lists, 15))
738	__CPROVER_assigns(pool->next;
739	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(lists);
740	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(pool->next))
741	/* --- No-op case: list 0 already holds all symbols, nothing changes. --- */
742	__CPROVER_ensures(
743	    (index == 0 && __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count) >= numsymbols) ==>
744	        (lists[index][1] == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]) &&
745	         lists[index][0] == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][0]) &&
746	         pool->next == __CPROVER_old(pool->next)))
747	/* --- Acting case: a new chain is appended; old last chain moves to slot 0. --- */
748	__CPROVER_ensures(
749	    !(index == 0 && __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count) >= numsymbols) ==>
750	        (lists[index][0] == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]) &&
751	         lists[index][1] == __CPROVER_old(pool->next)))
752	/* --- New leaf in list 0: weight/count/tail of the freshly created node. --- */
753	__CPROVER_ensures(
754	    (index == 0 && __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count) < numsymbols) ==>
755	        (lists[index][1]->count == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count) + 1 &&
756	         lists[index][1]->tail == 0 &&
757	         lists[index][1]->weight ==
758	             leaf_weight_at(leaves, __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count), numsymbols) &&
759	         pool->next == __CPROVER_old(pool->next) + 1))
760	/* --- New leaf inserted in a higher list (package keeps the lighter leaf). --- */
761	__CPROVER_ensures(
762	    (index > 0 &&
763	     __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count) < numsymbols &&
764	     __CPROVER_old(lists[index > 0 ? index - 1 : index][0]->weight) +
765	         __CPROVER_old(lists[index > 0 ? index - 1 : index][1]->weight) >
766	         leaf_weight_at(leaves, __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count), numsymbols)) ==>
767	        (lists[index][1]->count == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count) + 1 &&
768	         lists[index][1]->tail == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->tail) &&
769	         lists[index][1]->weight ==
770	             leaf_weight_at(leaves, __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count), numsymbols) &&
771	         pool->next == __CPROVER_old(pool->next) + 1))
772	/* --- Merge: combine the two lookahead chains of the previous list. --- */
773	__CPROVER_ensures(
774	    (index > 0 &&
775	     !(__CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count) < numsymbols &&
776	       __CPROVER_old(lists[index > 0 ? index - 1 : index][0]->weight) +
777	           __CPROVER_old(lists[index > 0 ? index - 1 : index][1]->weight) >
778	           leaf_weight_at(leaves, __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count), numsymbols))) ==>
779	        (lists[index][1]->count == __CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count) &&
780	         lists[index][1]->tail == __CPROVER_old(lists[index > 0 ? index - 1 : index][1]) &&
781	         lists[index][1]->weight ==
782	             __CPROVER_old(lists[index > 0 ? index - 1 : index][0]->weight) +
783	             __CPROVER_old(lists[index > 0 ? index - 1 : index][1]->weight)))
784	{
785	    Node *newchain;
786	    Node *oldchain;
787	    int lastcount = lists[index][1]->count; /* Count of last chain of list. */
788	
789	    if (index == 0 && lastcount >= numsymbols)
790	        return;
791	
792	    newchain = pool->next++;
793	    oldchain = lists[index][1];
794	
795	    /* These are set up before the recursive calls below, so that there is a list
796	    pointing to the new node, to let the garbage collection know it's in use. */
797	    lists[index][0] = oldchain;
798	    lists[index][1] = newchain;
799	
800	    if (index == 0)
801	    {
802	        /* New leaf node in list 0. */
803	        InitNode(leaves[lastcount].weight, lastcount + 1, 0, newchain);
804	    }
805	    else
806	    {

826	given counts. The bitlengths are limited by maxbits.
827	
828	The output is tailored for DEFLATE: symbols that never occur, get a bit length
829	of 0, and if only a single symbol occurs at least once, its bitlength will be 1,
830	and not 0 as would theoretically be needed for a single symbol.
831	
832	frequencies: The amount of occurrences of each symbol.
833	n: The amount of symbols.
834	maxbits: Maximum bit length, inclusive.
835	bitlengths: Output, the bitlengths for the symbol prefix codes.
836	return: 0 for OK, non-0 for error.

838	/* Bounds used only to give the size-less pointer arguments a concrete,
839	   verifiable extent and to keep the number of "used" symbols within the range
840	   accepted by the callee contracts (BoundaryPM/BoundaryPMFinal require
841	   1 <= numsymbols <= 8). They are properties of this contract, not of CBMC's
842	   command-line unwinding/depth flags. ZLLCL_MAX_N bounds the symbol count `n`
843	   (hence numsymbols <= n <= 8); ZLLCL_MAX_BITS bounds maxbits to a range for
844	   which `1 << maxbits` stays a well-defined positive int. */

846	#define ZLLCL_MAX_BITS 15
847	int ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths(
848	    const size_t *frequencies, int n, int maxbits, unsigned *bitlengths)
849	/* n symbols are processed; bounded so numsymbols stays within the callees'
850	   accepted range and the per-symbol loops/allocations have a concrete size. */
851	__CPROVER_requires(1 <= n && n <= ZLLCL_MAX_N)
852	/* maxbits is used as a shift amount (1 << maxbits) and as a list count. */
853	__CPROVER_requires(1 <= maxbits && maxbits <= ZLLCL_MAX_BITS)
854	/* frequencies[0 .. n-1] are read to count and weight the used symbols. The
855	   objects are pinned to ZLLCL_MAX_N elements (n <= ZLLCL_MAX_N, a sound
856	   over-allocation) so the success-condition quantifier in the second ensures
857	   below has a constant bound that CBMC can expand and a caller can discharge. */
858	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(frequencies, ZLLCL_MAX_N * sizeof(size_t)))
859	/* bitlengths[0 .. n-1] are the output array: every entry is written (to 0
860	   first, then the prefix-code lengths for used symbols). */
861	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(bitlengths, ZLLCL_MAX_N * sizeof(unsigned)))
862	/* The output array is the only caller-visible object written; the internal
863	   leaf/node/list buffers are freshly malloc'd (and freed) inside the call. */
864	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(bitlengths))
865	/* The function returns 0 on success and 1 on the two error conditions
866	   (too few maxbits for the symbols, or a weight needing more than 9 count
867	   bits); no other value is ever returned. */
868	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value == 0 || __CPROVER_return_value == 1)
869	/* Success is fully determined by the inputs: the function returns 1 only when
870	   either (a) the entry maxbits is too small to code the used symbols
871	   ((1 << maxbits) < numsymbols, and numsymbols <= n), or (b) some frequency is
872	   too large to leave 9 bits for the count (frequencies[k] >= 2^(WORDBITS-9)).
873	   If neither can occur, every path returns 0. This pins the return value so a
874	   caller that establishes both conditions (e.g. ZopfliCalculateBitLengths) can
875	   prove its own `assert(!error)`. */
876	__CPROVER_ensures(
877	    ( ((size_t)1 << __CPROVER_old(maxbits)) >= (size_t)n
878	      && __CPROVER_forall {
879	             size_t k;
880	             (k < (size_t)ZLLCL_MAX_N)

882	         } )

884	{
885	    NodePool pool;
886	    int i;
887	    int numsymbols = 0; /* Amount of symbols with frequency > 0. */
888	    int numBoundaryPMRuns;
889	    Node *nodes;
890	
891	    /* Array of lists of chains. Each list requires only two lookahead chains at
892	    a time, so each list is a array of two Node*'s. */
893	    Node *(*lists)[2];
894	
895	    /* One leaf per symbol. Only numsymbols leaves will be used. */
896	    Node *leaves = (Node *)malloc(n * sizeof(*leaves));
897	
898	    /* Initialize all bitlengths at 0. */
899	    for (i = 0; i < n; i++)
900	    {
901	        bitlengths[i] = 0;
902	    }
903	
904	    /* Count used symbols and place them in the leaves. */
905	    for (i = 0; i < n; i++)
906	    {
907	        if (frequencies[i])
908	        {
909	            leaves[numsymbols].weight = frequencies[i];
910	            leaves[numsymbols].count = i; /* Index of symbol this leaf represents. */
911	            numsymbols++;
912	        }
913	    }
914	
915	    /* Check special cases and error conditions. */
916	    if ((1 << maxbits) < numsymbols)
917	    {
918	        free(leaves);
919	        return 1; /* Error, too few maxbits to represent symbols. */
920	    }
921	    if (numsymbols == 0)
922	    {
923	        free(leaves);
924	        return 0; /* No symbols at all. OK. */
925	    }
926	    if (numsymbols == 1)
927	    {
928	        bitlengths[leaves[0].count] = 1;
929	        free(leaves);
930	        return 0; /* Only one symbol, give it bitlength 1, not 0. OK. */
931	    }
932	    if (numsymbols == 2)
933	    {
934	        bitlengths[leaves[0].count]++;
935	        bitlengths[leaves[1].count]++;
936	        free(leaves);
937	        return 0;
938	    }
939	
940	    /* Sort the leaves from lightest to heaviest. Add count into the same
941	    variable for stable sorting. */
942	    for (i = 0; i < numsymbols; i++)
943	    {
944	        if (leaves[i].weight >=
945	            ((size_t)1 << (sizeof(leaves[0].weight) * CHAR_BIT - 9)))
946	        {
947	            free(leaves);
948	            return 1; /* Error, we need 9 bits for the count. */
949	        }
950	        leaves[i].weight = (leaves[i].weight << 9) | leaves[i].count;
951	    }
952	    qsort(leaves, numsymbols, sizeof(Node), LeafComparator);
953	    for (i = 0; i < numsymbols; i++)
954	    {
955	        leaves[i].weight >>= 9;
956	    }
957	
958	    if (numsymbols - 1 < maxbits)
959	    {
960	        maxbits = numsymbols - 1;
961	    }
962	
963	    /* Initialize node memory pool. */
964	    nodes = (Node *)malloc(maxbits * 2 * numsymbols * sizeof(Node));
965	    pool.next = nodes;
966	
967	    lists = (Node * (*)[2]) malloc(maxbits * sizeof(*lists));
968	    InitLists(&pool, leaves, maxbits, lists);
969	
970	    /* In the last list, 2 * numsymbols - 2 active chains need to be created. Two
971	    are already created in the initialization. Each BoundaryPM run creates one. */
972	    numBoundaryPMRuns = 2 * numsymbols - 4;
973	    for (i = 0; i < numBoundaryPMRuns - 1; i++)
974	    {
975	        BoundaryPM(lists, leaves, numsymbols, &pool, maxbits - 1);
976	    }
977	    BoundaryPMFinal(lists, leaves, numsymbols, &pool, maxbits - 1);
978	
979	    ExtractBitLengths(lists[maxbits - 1][1], leaves, bitlengths);
980	
981	    free(lists);
982	    free(leaves);
983	    free(nodes);
984	    return 0; /* OK. */
985	}

The surviving mutant `<` → `<=` is **semantically equivalent**: the two operators differ only when `a == b`, and in that case the ternary returns `a` or `b` which are equal — the result is byte-for-byte identical. No specification can distinguish them, so 4/5 (0.80) is the maximum achievable kill score.
